data.title: Compass Gold Launches Resource-Definition Drilling at Massala Amid Permit Delay
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Compass Gold ( (TSE:CVB) ) has provided an announcement.

Compass Gold has commenced a two-phase, 2,200-metre diamond drilling program at its Massala prospect in Mali, aiming to define an initial gold resource over a 340-metre priority zone along the Tarabala Trend. The first phase includes 41 shallow holes totaling 1,300 metres, supported by QA/QC protocols and informed by earlier high-grade trenching and drilling results.

In parallel, the company has dispatched additional near-surface metallurgical samples for gravity and cyanide recovery testing, completed bulk sampling at two facilities, and is in talks with three parties on plant design and fabrication. Management continues to press Mali’s authorities for a delayed Small Mine Permit submitted in November 2025, signalling that Massala is being technically de-risked in anticipation of eventual project permitting and development.

More about Compass Gold

Compass Gold Corporation is a mineral exploration company focused on gold projects in Mali, West Africa. The company is advancing its Massala prospect along the 15-km Tarabala Trend, targeting near-surface gold mineralization that could support small-scale mining and future resource development.

Average Trading Volume: 278,854

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: C$27.87M

data.title: Sucro to Unveil 2025 Results as Expanded Sugar Network Takes Shape
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Sucro Limited ( (TSE:SUGR) ) has shared an announcement.

Sucro Limited will release its fourth quarter and full year 2025 financial results on April 16, 2026, before markets open, underscoring its efforts to keep investors informed as it scales its North American-focused sugar refining operations. Management, including the CEO and CFO, will host a conference call that morning to review performance and take questions, with a replay available through the end of April.

The scheduled results and call come as Sucro continues to ramp up its expanded refinery network in Canada and the U.S., a key plank in its strategy to leverage an integrated supply chain across the Americas. The update is likely to shed light on how recent capacity additions and supply chain initiatives are affecting growth, margins, and the company’s competitive position in the regional sugar market.

More about Sucro Limited

Sucro Limited is a growth-oriented, integrated sugar refiner operating throughout the Americas with a primary focus on serving the North American sugar market. The company runs a highly interconnected sugar supply chain, sourcing raw and refined sugar from Latin America and its own refineries, and delivering to customers across North America and the Caribbean.

Since its founding in 2014, Sucro has expanded through process innovation and supply chain re-engineering, building a broad production, sales, and sourcing network. Its footprint includes three cane sugar refineries, notably two recently opened facilities in Hamilton, Ontario, and University Park, Illinois, supported by offices in Miami, Mexico City, Cali, Sao Paulo, and Port of Spain.

Average Trading Volume: 328

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

The latest announcement is out from Frontera Energy ( (TSE:FEC) ).

Frontera Energy has filed and begun mailing its management information circular ahead of an April 30, 2026 special shareholder meeting to vote on a plan of arrangement under which Parex Resources, via a subsidiary, would acquire all of Frontera’s Colombian upstream assets, water treatment facility and palm oil plantation for US$500 million plus up to US$25 million in contingent consideration. Shareholders will also vote on a return of capital of up to C$647 million tied to the net cash proceeds from the transaction, and key investors and executives have entered support agreements, signaling strong backing for the deal and its proposed cash distribution to shareholders.

Technicals are a major offset, showing strong upward momentum and price strength versus key moving averages. Valuation is mixed due to a negative P/E (loss-making) and only a modest dividend yield.

data.title: Auric Minerals Launches New Financing and Moves to Settle Debt With Shares
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Auric Minerals Corp. ( (TSE:AUMC) ).

Auric Minerals Corp. has launched a new non-brokered private placement under the listed issuer financing exemption, aiming to raise between $1.5 million and $2 million through the sale of units priced at $0.21, each comprising one share and a warrant exercisable at $0.30 over 24 months. The financing, which replaces an expired prior offering and may include finder’s fees and warrants, is expected to close by late April 2026, subject to Canadian Securities Exchange approval, and is intended to support the company’s ongoing capital needs.

The company also plans to settle $288,000 of consulting and legal debt by issuing 1,028,570 common shares at a deemed price of $0.28, a move that will strengthen its balance sheet by reducing payables. Part of this settlement involves shares to a company controlled by CEO Chris Huggins, making it a related-party transaction, though Auric will rely on available exemptions from formal valuation and minority approval requirements under securities regulations.

More about Auric Minerals Corp.

Auric Minerals Corp. is a Canadian mineral exploration company based in Oakville, Ontario, focused on identifying, acquiring, exploring and developing mineral projects. The company holds 100% interests and options over more than 26,500 hectares of highly prospective uranium properties in Labrador’s Central Mineral Belt and Quebec, as well as the Goodeye gold property in British Columbia.

data.title: Barrick Slows Reko Diq Development Amid Rising Security Risks and Cost Pressures
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from Barrick Mining ( (TSE:ABX) ) is now available.

Barrick Mining has slowed development of its Reko Diq copper-gold project in Pakistan, extending a comprehensive review of the asset until mid-2027 amid escalating security risks in the country and wider region. The company continues to view Reko Diq as a valuable long-term project but will manage it with reduced capital spending while reassessing security, financing needs, project scope and timing.

Phase 1 of Reko Diq has been approved on this slower development basis, and Barrick expects significant increases to the previously disclosed multibillion-dollar capital budget and project timeline, including the prior goal of first production by the end of 2028. Despite the slowdown, Barrick plans to maintain active oversight of the project, honor its community and social commitments in-country, and work with joint venture partners as it monitors the evolving security landscape before providing further updates.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:ABX) stock is a Buy with a C$61.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Barrick Mining stock, see the TSE:ABX Stock Forecast page.

The score is driven primarily by strong financial performance (material margin expansion, improving leverage, and stronger cash generation) and a constructive earnings outlook with an enhanced shareholder-return framework. These positives are tempered by weak near-term technical momentum (oversold/bearish indicators) and operational risks flagged on the call (safety and PV recovery issues), while valuation appears reasonable but not a major catalyst.

The company operates and advances projects across 17 countries on five continents and is the largest gold producer in the United States, emphasizing responsible mining, strong partnerships and disciplined growth.

data.description:

Broadcom ( (AVGO) ) has issued an announcement.

On March 30, 2026, Broadcom announced that longtime finance chief Kirsten M. Spears will retire and resign as chief financial officer and chief accounting officer, effective June 12, 2026, and will remain as a consultant through March 15, 2027 under a transition agreement. The board appointed Alphabet veteran Amie Thuener as the next CFO, effective June 12, 2026, with employment starting May 4, 2026, under a compensation package that includes a $700,000 base salary, a sign-on bonus and significant equity awards tied to multi-year total shareholder return performance, underscoring Broadcom’s emphasis on continuity and performance-linked executive incentives.

The company serves markets including data center, networking, software, broadband, wireless, storage and industrial, with a focus on high-performance connectivity and infrastructure technologies.

On March 30, 2026, Broadcom announced that longtime finance chief Kirsten M. data.title: Southland Settles Convention Center Dispute, Seeks Financing Accord
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Southland Holdings ( (SLND) ) is now available.

On March 27, 2026, American Bridge Company entered into a settlement agreement with joint venture partner Clark/Lewis, its sureties, the Washington State Convention Center and its litigation counsel, resolving long-running disputes tied to the convention center expansion project and related litigation in King County Superior Court, which had led to a prior judgment of about $57.1 million that the sureties already paid with interest. Under the settlement, the sureties will pay roughly an additional $26.5 million to Clark/Lewis, Clark/Lewis will assign the judgment to the sureties and dismiss related claims with prejudice subject to limited reservations, and Southland says American Bridge’s financial obligations under the deal align with previously disclosed estimates, while repayment terms between the company and its sureties are still being negotiated under a forbearance that runs at least until March 27, 2027.

The settlement includes broad mutual releases among the parties and provides for dismissals of appeals related to the merits judgment once satisfaction is filed, though American Bridge is continuing to appeal court-ordered sanctions and certain obligations will remain in place. The outcome reduces legal uncertainty for Southland Holdings and its stakeholders by capping exposure on this contentious project within expected ranges, but it leaves the company reliant on reaching a long-term financing arrangement with its sureties, with no assurance that such an agreement will be finalized.

The most recent analyst rating on (SLND) stock is a Hold with a $1.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Southland Holdings stock, see the SLND Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on SLND Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SLND is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by sharp financial deterioration (declining revenue, deeply negative margins, and negative equity) and earnings-call disclosures of severe legacy-project/legal impacts with limited forward guidance. Technicals and valuation provide only mild support, with neutral momentum but a still-weak longer-term trend and a negative P/E due to ongoing losses.

To see Spark’s full report on SLND stock, click here.

More about Southland Holdings

Southland Holdings, Inc., through its wholly owned subsidiary American Bridge Company, operates in the construction and infrastructure sector, specializing in major civil works such as large-scale public projects. American Bridge has been involved in complex developments including the Washington State Convention Center expansion in Seattle, positioning the company within high-value, litigation-prone construction markets.

Average Trading Volume: 368,823

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $74.13M

data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Coterra Energy ( (CTRA) ) has issued an announcement.

On February 1, 2026, Coterra Energy Inc. agreed to be acquired by Devon Energy Corporation through a merger in which a Devon subsidiary will merge into Coterra, leaving Coterra as a wholly owned Devon unit. The transaction continues to advance, with the Hart-Scott-Rodino antitrust waiting period expiring at 11:59 p.m. Eastern Time on April 1, 2026, satisfying a key regulatory condition for closing.

Coterra and Devon filed required HSR notifications on March 2, 2026, and have also progressed securities regulatory steps, including an effective Form S-4 on March 26, 2026 and mailing of a joint proxy statement/prospectus starting March 30, 2026. The merger is expected to close in the second quarter of 2026, subject to remaining customary conditions, potentially reshaping both companies’ scale and competitive standing in the U.S. energy market.

The most recent analyst rating on (CTRA) stock is a Buy with a $40.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Coterra Energy stock, see the CTRA Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on CTRA Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CTRA is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by strong financial resilience (low leverage and solid cash generation) and a positive strategic catalyst from the announced Devon merger. This is tempered by overbought technical signals despite an uptrend, and a valuation that looks reasonable rather than clearly cheap.

To see Spark’s full report on CTRA stock, click here.

More about Coterra Energy

Coterra Energy Inc. is a Delaware-based independent energy company operating in the oil and natural gas sector. data.title: Starbucks Finalizes Boyu Joint Venture to Expand China
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Starbucks ( (SBUX) ) has provided an update.

On April 2, 2026, Starbucks announced the closing of its previously disclosed joint venture with Boyu Capital, completing a transaction first outlined in November 2025. The deal underscores Starbucks’ view of China as a critical long-term growth market and marks a strategic shift in how it operates there.

Under the agreement, funds managed by Boyu Capital acquired a 60% stake in Starbucks’ China retail operations, while Starbucks retained 40% ownership and continues to own and license the brand and intellectual property. Approximately 8,000 company-operated coffeehouses in China will transition to a licensed operating model, with both partners targeting a major expansion in store count over time.

Starbucks executives framed the joint venture as a way to accelerate disciplined expansion, improve profitability and deepen local relevance in China by combining Starbucks’ global brand with Boyu’s local expertise. For stakeholders, the move is intended to support faster market penetration, operating efficiency and brand strengthening in one of Starbucks’ most important and competitive markets, as the partners now move into the operational phase focused on growth and innovation.

The most recent analyst rating on (SBUX) stock is a Sell with a $84.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Starbucks stock, see the SBUX Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on SBUX Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SBUX is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weakened profitability versus prior years and elevated financial risk from high leverage/negative equity. This is partly offset by improving operating momentum and constructive FY26 guidance, along with strong technical trend signals. Valuation remains a notable headwind due to the very high P/E despite a moderate dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on SBUX stock, click here.

More about Starbucks

Starbucks Coffee Company, listed on Nasdaq as SBUX, is a global specialty coffee retailer and consumer-packaged goods company that responsibly sources and roasts high-quality arabica coffee. With more than 41,000 company-operated and licensed stores worldwide, it focuses on delivering the Starbucks Experience through its coffeehouses and branded products.

Since its founding in 1971, Starbucks has built a premier global coffeehouse footprint, combining in-store experiences with digital engagement and premium beverages, food, and merchandise. China is one of its most important growth markets, where it operates thousands of coffeehouses and seeks to strengthen its leadership position.

Boyu Capital, founded in 2011, is a leading alternative investment firm with Chinese roots and a global mandate, investing across private equity, public equity, infrastructure, and venture capital. It partners with consumer, technology, healthcare, and sustainable energy businesses, providing capital and strategic support to drive long-term value creation.

Average Trading Volume: 9,343,443

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $103B

data.title: Akanda Corp. Calls March 31, 2026 Special Meeting to Restructure Share Capital
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Akanda Corp ( (AKAN) ) has provided an announcement.

Akanda Corp. has called a special meeting of shareholders for March 31, 2026, in Toronto, with a simultaneous Zoom webcast for observers, to vote on significant amendments to its share capital structure. The meeting, later adjourned, will ask investors to approve the elimination of unissued Class A Special Shares, the mandatory conversion of all Class B Special Shares into common shares at the company’s discretion, and the subsequent deletion of Class B Special Shares from its authorized capital, moves that would simplify Akanda’s equity base and could streamline governance and future corporate actions for shareholders.

More about Akanda Corp

Akanda Corp., a foreign private issuer listed in the United States, is headquartered in Toronto, Canada. The company reports under Form 20-F to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission and maintains a common share structure with additional special share classes governed by its corporate articles.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell
